name: libregexp-assemble-perl
title: libregexp-assemble-perl
summary: Perl module to merge several regular expressions
description:
Perl module to merge several regular expressions
Regexp::Assemble takes an arbitrary number of regular expressions
and assembles them into a single regular expression (or RE) that
matches all that the individual REs match.
.
It is also possible to track the original patterns, so that you can
determine which, among the source patterns that form the assembled
pattern, was the one that caused the match to occur.

commands.name: regexp-assemble
commands.description: Assemble a list of regular expressions from a file

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use regexp-assemble because Perl module to merge several regular expressions.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use regexp-assemble as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `regexp-assemble -abcdfinprsStTuUvw file [...]` as documented upstream for regexp-assemble.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair regexp-assemble with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `regexp-assemble --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: regexp-assemble - Assemble a list of regular expressions from a file
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install libregexp-assemble-perl. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `regexp-assemble -abcdfinprsStTuUvw file [...]`. Perl module to merge several regular expressions.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Perl module to merge several regular expressions.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: regexp-assemble - Assemble a list of regular expressions from a file.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install libregexp-assemble-perl
Install with: sudo apt install libregexp-assemble-perl
